critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

C1 — LOW-VOLUME MAPE: 19.4% MAPE in bottom quintile disqualifies these stores for full autonomous perishable ordering.

C2 — NO OVERRIDE: No exception handling is an architectural gap that creates irreversible perishable waste or stockout risk.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (responding to critic):

C1 — Concede on performance grounds. 19.4% is above the perishable autonomy threshold. Tiered deployment addresses this.

C2 — Concede. Override or exception-handling mechanism is a minimum requirement for perishable goods management.
adjudication_raw: Full concession by defender on both points. Both issues require deployment modifications. Full-autonomy, no-override deployment for all 400 stores is not appropriate as proposed. Mixed verdict.
